assembly_instructions: Three-blade horizontal-axis wind turbine on a guyed tower. A concrete foundation pad supports a tapered tubular tower built in three stepped sections (widest at the bottom, narrowest at the top) with a small access door at the base. Three steel guy wires anchored in concrete blocks at 120° around the tower stabilize it against lateral wind loads. Atop the tower: a yaw bearing ring, the nacelle body with a rotor hub spinner at the front, three blades at 120° around the hub, and a tail boom ending in a broad tail vane. A small anemometer and wind vane sit on the nacelle roof. A DC cable runs down the outside of the tower to a ground-level battery shed + inverter box. Scale approx 0.3× real — scene is ≈7.5 m tall.

info.japanese_name: 50 kW オープンソース風力タービン
info.english_name: Open Source Ecology 50 kW Wind Turbine
info.summary: Horizontal-axis, three-blade wind turbine on a guyed tower. Part of OSE's Global Village Construction Set as the renewable-electricity prime mover.
info.description: The OSE 50 kW wind turbine is a community-scale, three-blade horizontal-axis wind turbine (HAWT) published as part of the Global Village Construction Set. The rotor captures wind via three composite blades bolted to a central hub; the hub turns a direct-drive DC generator housed in the nacelle at the top of a tall tower. A tail vane behind the nacelle aligns the rotor into the wind by weathervaning around a yaw bearing. A cup anemometer + wind vane on the nacelle roof feed the cut-in/cut-out controller. Direct-current output flows down a cable routed along the tower to a ground-level battery bank with a blocking diode and a small inverter that conditions the output. The tower is stabilized by three steel guy wires anchored in concrete blocks at 120° around the base. OSE's documentation is released under CC BY-SA 4.0.
info.operator: OSE community builders and off-grid installations
info.status: Design / small-scale builds; 50 kW class is the roadmap target

facts.label: Tower
facts.value: Three-section tapered tubular steel, guyed at 120° with three steel guy wires

facts.label: Control
facts.value: Passive yaw via tail vane; furling + anemometer cut-out

sources.title: OSE — Global Village Construction Set overview

parts.name: Concrete Foundation Pad
parts.role: Reinforced concrete pad anchoring the tower.

parts.name: Tower Section (Lower)
parts.role: Widest lower section of the tubular tower.

parts.name: Tower Section (Middle)
parts.role: Middle tapered tower section.

parts.name: Tower Section (Upper)
parts.role: Narrowest upper tower section — carries the yaw bearing.

parts.name: Tower Access Door
parts.role: Hinged access door at the base for internal cabling and anemometer maintenance.

parts.name: Yaw Bearing
parts.role: Large-diameter thrust bearing — lets the nacelle weathervane freely.

parts.name: Nacelle
parts.role: Sealed nacelle housing — direct-drive DC generator, yaw and brake hardware, controller.

parts.name: Rotor Hub Spinner
parts.role: Aerodynamic nose cone + structural hub at the front of the nacelle. Three blades bolt on at 120°.

parts.name: Blade #1 (top)
parts.role: Composite aerodynamic blade pointing straight up in the reference pose.

parts.name: Blade #2 (lower-left)
parts.role: Composite aerodynamic blade 120° around the rotor disc.

parts.name: Blade #3 (lower-right)
parts.role: Composite aerodynamic blade 240° around the rotor disc.

parts.name: Tail Boom
parts.role: Rear-projecting boom carrying the tail vane well behind the rotor plane.

parts.name: Tail Vane
parts.role: Broad vertical fin that yaws the rotor into the wind.

parts.name: Cup Anemometer
parts.role: Vertical cup-anemometer mast on the nacelle roof — reports wind speed for cut-in/cut-out.

parts.name: Wind-Direction Vane
parts.role: Small wind-direction vane alongside the anemometer.

parts.name: Guy Wire #1 (0°)
parts.role: Steel guy wire from a mid-tower collar to a concrete anchor on the +X side.

parts.name: Guy Wire #2 (120°)
parts.role: Guy wire to the −X anchor.

parts.name: Guy Wire #3 (240°)
parts.role: Guy wire to the +Z anchor (completes the three-way tie).

parts.name: Guy Anchor (+X)
parts.role: Concrete ground anchor at the +X guy termination.

parts.name: Guy Anchor (−X)
parts.role: Concrete ground anchor at the −X guy termination.

parts.name: Guy Anchor (+Z)
parts.role: Concrete ground anchor at the +Z guy termination.

parts.name: DC Downlead Cable
parts.role: Insulated DC cable running along the outside of the tower from the generator to the ground gear.

parts.name: Battery Shed
parts.role: Ground-level shed housing the battery bank and blocking diode.

parts.name: Inverter / Controller Box
parts.role: DC-DC / inverter + charge-controller mounted on the outside of the battery shed.
